title: (Sacramento, CA)

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2094 improves data collection on housing production for Extremely Low-Income Californians.",
    "The signed legislation requires local jurisdictions to report progress towards building Extremely Low-Income housing units separately from Very Low-Income housing units.",
    "Previously, Extremely Low-Income and Very Low-Income housing data were reported as a single data point.",
    "The lack of specificity in housing data undermined the state's ability to make informed policy and funding decisions.",
    "AB 2094 provides necessary data for local jurisdictions and the California Department of Housing Community Development to effectively house vulnerable residents.",
    "The average Californian experiencing homelessness earns 14% of the area median income (AMI).",
    "Understanding housing data is essential to effectively address the housing and homelessness crises in California."
  ]
}
```
